element_introduction_order.element: Blue Planet water mechanics and enemies

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform zones between enemy encounters
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters with varied attack patterns
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive introduction of enemy types and hazard complexity across planetary stages, building toward final confrontation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational platforming and basic enemy introduction on initial planetary stages
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating hazard complexity with stage-specific mechanics (water navigation, ice platforms, electrical obstacles)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Combination of multi-part enemies (Triborg splitting), vertical climbing challenges, and aerial combat patterns
notable_spikes: Cell stage introduction of electrical hazard timing puzzles
Salvage Chute with Triborg multi-part enemy mechanics
Final level aerial enemy patterns and climbing sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured around distinct planetary environments (Yellow Planet, Blue Planet, Cell, Salvage Chute, Final level) each introducing location-specific enemy types and environmental hazards
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Yellow Planet (tank enemies, exploding rocks), Blue Planet (water, ice blocks)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels feature navigable platforms; middle stages introduce water mechanics and ice platforming; later stages add electrical obstacles requiring precise timing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Blue Planet ice blocks → Cell electrical zappers

notable_level_patterns.description: Progression from stationary/simple enemies to multi-part enemies (Triborg splits into three) to aerial attack patterns
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic enemies → Triborg (Salvage Chute) → Flying ships (Final level)

sources_used: tcrf - Bucky O'Hare (NES) technical breakdown with level/enemy data
